_instructions: Add titles here. Set 'done': true when reviewed. 'score' and 'notes' are optional pre-logs of your gut reaction — if set, the AI will write to that score. This is the ONLY queue file the daily review bot reads (data/queue.json) — do not create or edit a queue.json at the repo root, it is ignored by the script.

movies.title: They Will Kill You
movies.notes: I loved the R-rated parts but I disliked the rest, didn't make sense, felt too bizarre, constant action sure, but it couldn't keep my attention throughout, I felt more like I wanted to grab my phone than look at my monitor.

movies.title: Good Luck, Have Fun, Don't Die
movies.notes: I loved the twist at the end, everything else yeah maybe in parts a bit convuluted but I enjoyed the wacky stuff of it all, and Sam Rockwell played the role of a lifetime.

movies.title: Alien 3
movies.notes: Actually quite decent, depending on which version you see — the director's cut, the Assembly Cut, is the one to watch. It has some updated CGI but the CGI looked awful regardless. This is the real end of Ripley, before she gets brought back in Resurrection — she dies going into the lava at the end. The different setting, the prison, worked for the movie overall, and I liked the characters. The CGI is what holds it back from scoring higher.

movies.title: Alien: Resurrection
movies.notes: This is where they resurrect Ripley, and the whole thing felt weird — she's more like a hybrid now. I like Sigourney Weaver as an actress, no doubt, but I think the franchise could honestly work without her at this point. Same old, same thing. I did like some of the action, but overall it just didn't work for me.

movies.title: Prometheus
movies.notes: An origin story, and I like the idea of it, but the execution was kinda meh. If it had leaned into a story not centered on Ripley for once, I think it could've landed even better.

movies.title: The Evil Dead
movies.notes: A really goofy but gory movie that was extremely low budget but it had heart and passion.

movies.title: Evil Dead 2
movies.notes: Way goofier, return of Bruce Campbell, kind of a re-telling of the first one due to a copyright dispute which is kinda bizarre, more funnier, Bruce Campbell is amazing as Ash, loved the creepy stop motion stuff.

movies.title: Army of Darkness
movies.notes: Continues Ash's story but he gets transported to medieval times, funny, new setting, character development. I like the more negative ending when he wakes up but slept too long and the entire world is in ruins, however we know that they went with the mall ending, since years later we get Ash vs Evil Dead the TV series.

movies.title: Evil Dead
movies.notes: I prefer the unrated version since it has certain scenes extended, especially when she pops her head up from the basement and starts singing, very creepy. Unfortunately this is also the start of all main women becoming the protagonists, which I think the Evil Dead franchise should move back to a male one surviving once again. Overall I think it's a solid more grounded take of the movies and its a remake/reboot of the franchise, with better makeup and all. Still, I love the original trilogy more.

movies.title: Evil Dead Rise
movies.notes: Was a nice uncanny valley type of movie, I loved the setting being in an apartment, I loved the possession and the way the main lady was acting as a deadite, but it lost me towards the ending where the kids and their mom become one weird monster-esque thing. I love creative stuff but even for the Evil Dead franchise I thought it was too much, if they stuck with them being 3 deadites surrounding the girl with the chainsaw, it would not only be more spookier but they'd also have a chance of killing her and getting away with it or turning her, I would've loved a twist like that but apparently Evil Dead Rise plays it safe, which I find unfortunate, I like twists. And again just like Evil Dead 2013, it has a female protagonist.

movies.title: Saw
movies.notes: Amazing. Great concept, great acting, low budget but it worked perfectly. One of the best horror debuts in years — the concept alone earns its place in the genre.

movies.title: Saw II
movies.notes: Became more gruesome and disgusting — loved it. A worthy follow-up that dials up the nastiness without losing the grip of the original.

movies.title: Saw III
movies.notes: Same old same old with some things here and there. Jigsaw dying of brain cancer on the table while Amanda runs the traps is the most interesting thread — the rest is starting to feel familiar.

movies.title: Saw IV
movies.notes: Not much to say. It's there, it does Saw things, but it doesn't leave much of an impression either way.

movies.title: Saw V
movies.notes: More of the same, unfortunately. The deaths are still there and I still like them, but everything around them is stale. The franchise is running on fumes.

movies.title: Saw VI
movies.notes: A bit better than the last few — the health insurance angle gives Jigsaw's philosophy an actual target for once. Nothing amazing but a slight step back up.

movies.title: Saw 3D: The Final Chapter
movies.notes: Didn't know what it was supposed to be and it didn't work for me. Marketed as the definitive end, shot in 3D, brought back Dr. Gordon — none of it added up to anything satisfying.

movies.title: Jigsaw
movies.notes: Soft reboot after seven years away — kinda okay but meh overall. The mystery of who's running the game is fine but it doesn't reignite anything.

movies.title: Spiral: From the Book of Saw
movies.notes: Read the reviews after years of disappointment, watched 30 minutes and said no to myself. Never finished it. From what I saw I wouldn't rate it higher than a 4 — it wants to be a crime thriller more than a Saw film and fails at both.

movies.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street
movies.notes: Fantastic. Something completely new at the time — atmospheric, disturbing, and genuinely original. The bathtub scene became an iconic shot to me personally, maybe not mainstream pop culture iconic but it stuck with me in a way few horror images do.

movies.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street 2: Freddy's Revenge
movies.notes: Kind of an extension of the first one — Freddy possessing the boy from the inside rather than invading dreams was a different angle but it didn't do much for me. Some nice deaths but that's about it.

movies.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street 3: Dream Warriors
movies.notes: This one was a genuine surprise. Dream Warriors works really well — highly entertaining, great deaths, Nancy coming back as a counsellor was a smart move. This should've been the definitive sequel and in many ways it is.

movies.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street 4: The Dream Master
movies.notes: Did not like it at all. Picks off the Dream Warriors survivors one by one and replaces them with a new lead — it loses everything that made the previous entry work.

movies.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street 5: The Dream Child
movies.notes: Didn't know what to make of this one. Freddy using an unborn child to enter the real world felt weird — the pregnancy angle is a dark premise but it doesn't go anywhere interesting with it.

movies.title: Freddy's Dead: The Final Nightmare
movies.notes: Kinda meta, which is why I liked it — there's a self-awareness to it that works here more than it has any right to. Different Freddy look as well, which landed. Played for laughs in parts and it actually worked. An entertaining send-off even if it's a strange one.

movies.title: Hellraiser
movies.notes: Disgusting deaths, genuinely unique, grotesque in the best way. Hell as a concept felt real and horrifying here. One of the most distinctive horror films ever made.

movies.title: Hellbound: Hellraiser II
movies.notes: Feels like a bigger budget from the looks of it. Interesting continuation — held my attention and built on the original well enough to be worth watching.

movies.title: Hellraiser III: Hell on Earth
movies.notes: Disgusting deaths but that's about it. The franchise starts going stale here — the kills are there but nothing else holds it up.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Bloodline
movies.notes: Gruesome deaths kept me engaged but the story didn't interest me at all. The kills are doing all the heavy lifting and it's not enough.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Inferno
movies.notes: Interesting premise — felt like early Saw to me, that same kind of detective-in-a-trap setup. The cop with hooks in his face was something else. A step above the previous two entries but not by a huge margin.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Hellseeker
movies.notes: Can't fully recall it, which tells you everything. Wasn't amused. Went in one eye and out the other.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Deader
movies.notes: So cheap I felt like I went to Hades myself during the film. Production value is non-existent.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Hellworld
movies.notes: Interesting premise on paper — completely flatlined in execution. Two Hellraiser films in the same year and neither one had any business existing.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Revelations
movies.notes: So cheap in every possible way — the acting, the production, all of it. One of the worst entries in any horror franchise I've seen. A new low.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Judgment
movies.notes: Not as forgettable as the entries before it — they clearly tried this time. But trying and succeeding are different things and this still falls flat.

movies.title: Hellraiser
movies.notes: First time a woman as the lead, which I didn't expect, and it landed somewhat. Absolutely gruesome — a genuine attempt to go back to the roots of the original with some nice practical effects. A great effort and return for the franchise even if it doesn't fully get there.

tv.title: The Haunting of Hill House
tv.notes: The jump scares genuinely got to me — the coffin scene where the camera pans around and the girl suddenly appears, the girl in the car, the ghost with the cane hitting the floor while floating. All hit hard. Great performances across the board. One of the best haunted house stories put to screen.

tv.title: The Haunting of Bly Manor
tv.notes: After Hill House it felt more of the same and slipped into cliché territory. Still had great scenes and strong performances that kept me engaged throughout, but it never matches what came before it.

tv.title: The Fall of the House of Usher
tv.notes: Slow start — the beginning was meh — but the story builds and gets better as it progresses. Never reaches Hill House in terms of execution but it finds its footing and delivers enough to be worth it.

tv.title: Alien: Earth
tv.notes: Started out quite okay, could've gone in a lot of directions, but then it commits to this whole child-genius-consciousness-uploaded-into-synthetic-bodies angle, fighting over sick kids turned into extremely powerful synths. By the end one of them is basically a Santa figure who can talk to the alien — one of the weirdest things I've ever seen on screen. Season one, 4/10.

games.title: Alien: Isolation
games.notes: Why not a perfect 10 you ask? At certain times especially on the hardest difficulty, I find certain things a bits bit more annoying, yeah you got less health makes sense but the Alien legit hears everything, even from a mile away and it can sprint so fast even when you don't hear it, apart from that when you get the flamthrower sometimes the Alien doesn't get bothered by it and still kill you, very frustrating moments, thats why I give it the grade I give it.
